findings.description: Stateless EventStore architecture with DAPR actor placement for automatic distribution. Kubernetes Deployment with configurable replicas, HPA-ready resource limits, health probes for traffic management. Each replica can serve any tenant without session affinity.
evidence: Stateless design: no in-process session data, all state in DAPR state store

findings.description: Backend portability via DAPR abstraction: Redis (dev), PostgreSQL (prod), Cosmos DB (scale). Composite key pattern ({tenant}||{domain}||{aggregateId}) enables natural data partitioning. Snapshot strategy bounds replay time regardless of event count (NFR19). No direct DB calls — all access through DaprClient.
evidence: deploy/dapr/statestore-cosmosdb.yaml (Cosmos DB backend with partition keys)
PRD NFR29: Zero code changes for backend swap (validated architecture)
recommendations: MEDIUM: Test Cosmos DB partition key strategy under multi-tenant load
OPTIONAL: Document event archival strategy for long-lived aggregates

findings.description: Dual-layer rate limiting (per-tenant + per-consumer), DAPR pub/sub for async event distribution, per-aggregate backpressure with HTTP 429 (FR67), SignalR for real-time notifications. Dynamic topic provisioning for new tenants without restart.

Target: 10+ tenants per instance (NFR18) with full isolation and no cross-tenant performance interference. Architecture supports this via per-tenant rate limits, actor-level isolation, and DAPR policy scoping. However, cross-tenant performance isolation is UNTESTED — no evidence of concurrent multi-tenant load testing.
evidence: PRD NFR18: 10+ tenants per instance target
Per-tenant rate limiting with configurable overrides (TenantPermitLimits)
recommendations: HIGH: Run multi-tenant load test validating NFR18 (10+ tenants, no cross-tenant interference)
MEDIUM: Test per-tenant rate limit fairness under contention

findings.description: Target: 10K+ active aggregates per instance (NFR17) without degradation. Architecture supports this via DAPR actor placement and snapshot strategy. However, actor memory footprint at 10K+ scale is UNTESTED. Snapshot effectiveness for state rehydration is theoretically sound but unvalidated.
evidence: DAPR actor placement distributes across replicas
NO memory profiling at 10K+ actor scale
recommendations: HIGH: Profile memory and GC at 10K active actors
MEDIUM: Validate snapshot strategy prevents memory growth

findings.description: Aspire publishers for 3 targets (Docker Compose, Kubernetes Helm, Azure Container Apps). Semantic-release with NuGet autopublish. CI/CD with 5 GitHub Actions workflows. Dockerfiles with Alpine base, non-root user. Rolling update strategy with health probes.
recommendations: OPTIONAL: Implement blue-green or canary deployment strategy
OPTIONAL: Add GitOps (ArgoCD/Flux) for declarative deployment

priority_actions: HIGH: Run multi-tenant load test (10+ tenants, NFR18)
HIGH: Profile 10K+ active actor memory footprint (NFR17)
MEDIUM: Validate Cosmos DB partition strategy under load
summary: Scalability posture is MEDIUM risk. Architecture is well-designed for scale (stateless, DAPR actor placement, backend portability, rate limiting) but quantitative scaling targets (10K aggregates, 10 tenants, 100 cmd/sec) are UNTESTED. Horizontal scaling and deployability are PASS. Key risk: no load test evidence for scaling limits.
